response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which made his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David collected three times as many as Sophia meaning Sophia had 13 blueberries. Together they had 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. This total equals 76 blueberries. 

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch required 9 blueberries so they made 8 batches with 4 blueberries left over. Each batch was sold for $5 so they earned 8 times 5 dollars. This calculation gives them $40 from selling the muffins. 

***

The total number of blueberries collected was 76. Divided by the 9 needed per batch they made 8 full batches. The leftover blueberries were not used in the muffins. The money made from the muffins was $40. This amount represents the earnings from their collection. 

***

Their efforts led to a successful sale of muffins. The money earned from the batches was $40. The blueberries were the foundation of their success. The youth of their venture was marked by their hard work.
